_comment: Example config for the Calendar Agent (TASK-29). Copy this to ~/.config/agent-manager/calendar-agent.json and replace every <PLACEHOLDER>. JSON has no comments, so explanatory keys are prefixed with _comment. They are ignored by loadConfig(). Full step-by-step setup: see docs/SETUP.md.
_comment_aiCalendarId: ID of the dedicated AI calendar — the ONLY calendar the agent may write to. The write tools (create_event/update_event/delete_event) HARD-ENFORCE this: a write to any other calendarId is refused, and if this key is unset the agent refuses ALL writes (safe default — it only reads + escalates). Do NOT rely on the calendar's name; the model must use this exact ID. Find it via the `list_calendars` tool, or in Google Calendar: Settings → (select the AI calendar) → Integrate calendar → Calendar ID. It usually ends with @group.calendar.google.com.
_comment_calendar_gmail: Calendar + Gmail are NOT configured here. They are exposed as IN-PROCESS SDK MCP tools (servers `calendar` + `gmail`) built by the host over the raw Google REST APIs (calendar/v3, gmail/v1). We do NOT use the official Google remote MCP servers — they are gated behind the Google Workspace Developer Preview and reject personal @gmail accounts. The only thing you provide is OAuth credentials at ~/.config/agent-manager/google-oauth.json (client_id, client_secret, refresh_token); the host's token manager exchanges them for short-lived access tokens at runtime. See docs/SETUP.md, Part 2.
_comment_mcpServers: Only the local WhatsApp stdio server is configured here. It is the lharries/whatsapp-mcp Python server, launched via uv. The Go bridge (whatsapp-bridge/main.go) runs SEPARATELY and must be running + QR-authenticated first. Paths below are the real verified ones for this machine.
_comment_whitelist: Filters inputs BEFORE the agent reasons (AC#3). WhatsApp groups are matched by name (case-insensitive). Gmail passes if the From address contains any sender substring OR the message carries an allowed label. Empty arrays = deny-all. Replace the placeholders with your real values.
